title: NVIDIA AI Workbench Project Spec
description: Schema for .project/spec.yaml in an NVIDIA AI Workbench project

specVersion.description: Spec format version. Always 'v2' for current projects.

specMinorVersion.description: Minor version of the spec format.

name.description: Project name. Used as the directory name and display name.

image.description: Container image name for this project (e.g. 'project-my-project').

layout.description: Project directory structure. Each entry defines a directory and how it is tracked in git.

path.description: Directory path relative to the project root (e.g. 'code/').

storage.description: Git tracking strategy. 'git' = normal tracking, 'gitlfs' = large file storage, 'gitignore' = not tracked.

base.description: Base container image and its properties. Managed by Workbench — do not edit package_managers.installed_packages manually; use 'nvwb add package' instead.

build_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the base image build.

name.description: Human-readable name of the base environment.

cuda_version.description: CUDA toolkit version available in the image (e.g. '12.0'). Empty string for CPU-only images.

user_info.description: Custom container user identity. Leave empty to use Workbench defaults.

package_managers.description: Package managers available in the container. The installed_packages lists are managed by Workbench — use 'nvwb add package' to modify them.

binary_path.description: Absolute path to the package manager binary.

installed_packages.description: Managed by Workbench — do not edit manually, use 'nvwb add package'. Note: this list is informational and may not accurately reflect what is actually installed; inspect the running container directly (e.g. 'pip freeze') for ground truth.

apps.description: Applications built into the base image (e.g. JupyterLab). Can be overridden in execution.apps.

compose_file_path.description: Path to a Docker Compose file relative to the project root. Empty string means no compose services.

execution.description: Runtime configuration — apps, resources, environment variables, and mounts.

apps.description: Custom applications defined by this project. An app here with the same name as a base app overrides it.

requested.description: Number of GPUs to allocate. 0 for CPU-only.

sharedMemoryMB.description: Shared memory allocation in megabytes. 0 uses the system default.

secrets.description: Declarations of required sensitive environment variables. Names and descriptions only — values are never stored in the spec. Set values with: nvwb create environment-variable VAR value --is-sensitive

description.description: Human-readable description of what this variable is for.

type.description: 'project' = the project directory (always /project/), 'host' = a host filesystem path, 'volume' = a named Docker volume, 'tmp' = tmpfs.

target.description: Absolute path inside the container where this mount appears.

options.description: Mount options. Common values: 'rw', 'ro', 'volumeName=<name>'. Host mount sources are configured separately via 'nvwb configure mounts'.

app.description: An application that Workbench can start, stop, and health-check.

name.description: Unique identifier for this app. Used in 'nvwb start <name>'.

type.description: 'jupyterlab', 'vs-code', 'cursor', or 'custom'. 'cursor' and 'vs-code' are native IDE types managed by Workbench.

class.description: 'webapp' = HTTP service with a browser UI, 'process' = background process, 'native' = desktop/IDE app (e.g. Cursor, VS Code) managed by Workbench.

start_command.description: Shell command to start the app. Note: when using pipes or subshells, environment variables like PROXY_PREFIX must be explicitly passed (e.g. PROXY_PREFIX=$PROXY_PREFIX python app.py | tee log).

stop_command.description: Shell command to stop the app.

health_check_command.description: Shell command that exits 0 when the app is ready.

user_msg.description: Message shown to the user after the app starts.

logfile_path.description: Path to the app's log file inside the container. If empty, stdout is captured.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for health check to pass. 0 means no timeout.

icon_url.description: URL of an icon image for this app.

webapp_options.description: Options for webapp-class apps. Not used for process or native apps.

autolaunch.description: Whether to open the app in a browser automatically on start.

port.description: Port the app listens on inside the container.

trim_prefix.description: Whether Workbench's reverse proxy should strip the URL prefix before forwarding to the app. Set true if the app does not understand the proxy prefix.

url.description: Static URL to open for this app. Must include a trailing slash (e.g. 'http://localhost:8080/'). Use this OR url_command, not both.

url_command.description: Shell command that outputs the URL to open. Used when the URL is dynamic (e.g. Jupyter token URLs). Use this OR url, not both.

wait_until_finished.description: If true, the Workbench UI waits for the process to complete before returning. Useful for one-off tasks like model downloads.
